trade.summary: Poland-US trade is substantial and growing. Poland exports machinery, automotive components, electronics, furniture, chemicals, and food to the US. The US exports aircraft, military equipment, LNG, software, and pharmaceuticals to Poland. The US is one of Poland's most important trading partners outside the EU. US LNG exports to Poland have grown significantly as part of Poland's energy diversification strategy.

diplomatic.status: strategic partnership — one of the closest US bilateral relationships in Europe
diplomatic.summary: The US-Poland relationship is one of the closest bilateral partnerships in NATO Europe. Poland is considered the most reliable US ally on the continent by many US policymakers, given its consistent support for strong Atlantic solidarity, high defense spending, and unequivocal backing of Ukraine. Poland has hosted US presidents, signed bilateral defense cooperation agreements, and positions itself as America's indispensable partner in Central-Eastern Europe. Some friction occurred during Trump's first term over burden-sharing rhetoric, but the underlying alliance is deep. Poland has consistently supported US positions on Russia, China, and Middle East.
recent_incidents: Ongoing discussions on permanent US military basing in Poland (Fort Trump concept)
US-Poland nuclear energy cooperation agreements signed
Poland publicly critical of any US engagement with Russia without Ukrainian involvement

military.summary: The US-Poland military relationship is the most significant bilateral defence relationship in Poland's history. The US has ~10,000+ troops permanently stationed or rotated through Poland, including armored brigade combat teams, air force elements, and intelligence assets. Poland has purchased F-35s, HIMARS, Abrams tanks, Patriot air defense, and other US systems worth $30+ billion. Permanent US basing discussions are ongoing. Poland hosts V Corps (US Army) forward headquarters. Joint exercises are extensive and frequent. Poland has more US forward-deployed capability than any other NATO ally.
military.alliance_status: NATO allies — premier bilateral defence partnership in NATO Europe

financial.summary: US companies are major investors in Poland. Google, Microsoft, Amazon Web Services, Intel have major data center and R&D investments in Poland. US private equity has invested heavily in Polish logistics, real estate, and technology. Polish companies increasingly list on US markets and seek US venture capital. The US-Poland Investment Fund has supported bilateral commercial engagement.

energy.summary: Energy is a growing pillar of the relationship. Poland was an early purchaser of US LNG (Polskie LNG terminal in Swinoujscie), and US LNG is now a major component of Poland's gas supply. US companies (Westinghouse) are contracted to build Poland's first nuclear power plants. US energy equipment and services companies are active in Polish power sector modernization.

scientific.summary: Strong scientific partnership across engineering, IT, life sciences, and social sciences. Polish universities have deep partnerships with US research institutions (MIT, Stanford, University of California system). Polish-American diaspora (Polish-Americans number ~10 million) maintains cultural and scientific bridges. NSF and Polish NCN run joint research programs. The US is the top destination for Polish researchers abroad.
